item.title: Version 0.9.4
item.description:
What's New in 0.9.4

  • Single-monitor fix — The Record confirmation panel no longer disappears off-screen after you unplug an external display. Stale saved positions that fall outside the current screens are dropped automatically.
  • Centered preflight — The confirmation modal now appears centered on the active screen so it's easy to spot. Once recording starts, the pill returns to its usual spot above the Dock.

item.title: Version 0.9.3
item.description:
What's New in 0.9.3

  • Tooltip polish — Hover tooltips no longer clip the last line behind nearby controls. Fixed a SwiftUI sizing bug in the tooltip background and trimmed the Record button's hint to fit on a single line.

item.title: Version 0.9.2
item.description:
What's New in 0.9.2

  • Global shortcut fix — Option+Shift+R (record) and Option+Shift+S (screenshot) now work reliably on macOS Tahoe (26.x). Rewrote the shortcut layer on top of Carbon's RegisterEventHotKey, the same API used by Spotlight and Raycast, which sidesteps the Tahoe TCC ghost-binding bug that broke CGEvent taps even when Input Monitoring was granted.
  • One less permission — Memois no longer needs Input Monitoring. The Permissions screen drops to just Microphone and Screen Recording.

item.title: Version 0.9.1
item.description:
What's New in 0.9.1

  • Recording reliability — Recordings now keep going even if Screen Recording isn't granted (mic-only fallback) instead of failing the whole session.
  • Permission UX — Enable buttons for Screen Recording and Input Monitoring now open System Settings directly. The app no longer locks itself in an error state when only Screen Recording / Input Monitoring is missing.
  • Processed view — Recordings sent to Claude Code or Codex are automatically marked as processed and visually fade in the list. New row layout puts duration and status on the same line as the title.
  • Better recording row — Whole button area is clickable (not just the icon), pointer cursor on hover, and rich tooltips on every action.
  • Delete confirmations — Trash and Clear All now ask before destroying audio, transcripts and screenshots.
  • Persistent debug log — `/tmp/memois-aai.log` keeps a session banner and audio-pipeline diagnostics across launches for easier support.

item.title: Version 0.9.0
item.description:
What's New in 0.9.0

  • Live subtitles — Real-time transcription panel that streams committed turns and partial text while you record, with detected language badge and live diagnostics (bytes sent, peak level, last event).
  • Microphone preflight — New pre-recording check verifies the selected input device, permissions, and signal level before you hit record, so you don't lose a meeting to a muted mic.
  • Floating subtitles panel — Dedicated SwiftUI surface for the live transcript that you can keep visible while focused on other windows.
  • Better internal logging — Centralized debug log makes it easier to diagnose audio pipeline and transcription issues from support reports.

item.title: Version 0.8.1
item.description:
What's New in 0.8.1

  • Dedicated Projects workspace — Manage Claude Code and Codex projects from a new sidebar section instead of buried inline controls.
  • Per-project custom prompts — Save a reusable prompt template for each project and insert meeting variables with one click.
  • Transcript always included — Custom prompts stay concise while Memois automatically appends the full meeting transcript when it sends context to the agent.
  • More reliable Codex launches — The app now inherits your login-shell PATH so Codex starts correctly from the macOS app, with clearer error logging if something still fails.

item.title: Version 0.8.0
item.description:
What's New in 0.8.0

  • Send transcripts to Claude Code or Codex — Run either coding agent directly from a recording, choose a saved project, and keep the response alongside the meeting.
  • Better headless agent setup — Configure saved project directories, override CLI executable paths when auto-detection fails, and reveal the transcript directly in Finder.
  • Cleaner transcript workflow — Real-time speaker names, speaker filtering, improved action buttons, and smoother agent actions in the recordings list.
  • More resilient recordings — Auto-recover orphaned recordings on launch, allow back-to-back recordings while the previous save finishes, and show progress while repairing audio.

item.title: Version 0.7.0
item.description:
What's New in 0.7.0

  • Fix stuck transcriptions — Transcriptions stuck in "processing" after app restart are now automatically recovered. Retry button available as safety net.
  • Faster transcript viewer — Large transcriptions (1h+) now open instantly with lazy rendering, background audio loading, and async data loading.
  • Smoother speaker editing — Renaming speakers no longer causes lag on large transcriptions. Names apply on Enter instead of every keystroke.

item.title: Version 0.6.0
item.description:
What's New in 0.6.0

  • Screenshots during recording — Camera button + global shortcut (⌥⇧S) to capture screen regions while recording. Screenshots saved in the recording folder with timestamps.
  • Transcript search — Search bar to find text in transcripts with match highlighting and navigation
  • Audio-text sync — Click any utterance to jump the audio to that exact moment. Active utterance highlighted during playback.
  • Timestamps per utterance — Each speaker turn shows its timestamp for easy navigation
  • Enhanced Copy — Copied transcript now includes recording title and screenshot file paths

item.title: Version 0.5.1
item.description:
What's New in 0.5.1

  • Check for Updates in Settings — New button in Settings to check for and install updates

item.title: Version 0.5.0
item.description:
What's New in 0.5.0

  • Auto-update support — Memois now checks for updates automatically using Sparkle
  • Repair button — Recover recordings when chunks exist but the merged audio file is missing
  • Fix: recording file not found — Fixed race condition when stopping and immediately starting a new recording
